I CERTIFY UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY UNDER THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF WASHINGTON THAT ALL STATEMENTS MADE HEREIN ARE TRUE AND ACCURATE AND THAT I AM ENTERING MY AUTHORIZED USER ID AND PASSWORD TO AUTHENTICATE IT (RCW 9A. 72.085) . Electronically Signed: Yes Signature: Tyson T Brown 2D5 Date: 9/9/20
Chehalis/Lewis/Washington
SUPPLEMENTAL NARRATIVE:
Name: Robb S Date: 14:10:38 09/09/20 SUPPLEMENTAL / S. ROBB / 09-09-2020
AGENCY CASE NUMBERS : Grays Harbor County Sheriff's Office: 20-16695 Lewis County Sheriff's Office: 20C11252 Thurston County Sheriff's Office: 20-4433 Washington State Patrol: 20-014825
EVIDENCE : I took 104 photos with my agency issued iPhone.
PHOTOS : Please see the attached photo log for more information.
SUPPLEMENTAL NARRATIVE : On Thursday, September 03, 2020, at approximately 2015 hours, I received a phone call from Detective Sergeant Kevin Engelbertson notifying me of a Region 3 Critical Incident Investigation Team (CIIT) call out. He told me there was an officer involved shooting (OIS) incident in Thurston County that occurred at 7601 3rd Way SE, Lacey, WA 98503. He told me to meet at the Lacey Police Department, located at 420 College St SE, Lacey, WA 98503.
At approximately 2110 hours, I arrived at the Lacey Police Department and met up with other investigators from the CIIT. Detective Mickey Hamilton, with the Thurston County Sheriff's Office, briefed us on the events that occurred. He told us a suspect in a Portland, OR homicide had been tracked to an apartment at the 3rd Way SE address by members of the US Marshall Task Force. We were told the suspect in this case, Michael Reinoehl, had been seen putting a rifle case into a silver colored Honda passenger car and then got into a beige colored Volkswagen passenger car. We were told during the contact with Mr. Reinoehl, multiple Task Force Officers had fired their weapons and Mr. Reinoehl was deceased.
Due to the lack of available resources over the Labor Day holiday weekend, additional law enforcement personnel, who were not assigned to the CIIT, were called in and paired with CIIT members. I was paired with Detective Jordan Goss, who is with the Thurston County Sheriff's Office. Prior to speaking with any of the shooting and witness officers, we were told that none of the officers would be providing statements.
Detective Goss and I were introduced to Pierce County Deputy James Oleole, who
rplwdir.x1
